title: Enceladus plumes
script_summary: Explore the secrets of Enceladus plumes and what they reveal about our solar system.

segments.voiceover: Science has a clearer story about Enceladus plumes than social media does.
Enceladus, a moon of Saturn, shoots out icy plumes into space. But why?
Scientists believe these plumes come from a subsurface ocean, hinting at possible life.
Data from the Cassini mission showed organic molecules in the plumes. Fascinating, right?
So, Enceladus isn't just a frozen moon. It holds clues about our universe's potential for life!
Curious about other space mysteries? Like and follow for more discoveries!
